explanation: think Tubular bells or hand bells. See also the BELL category for non-musical uses. BELL-GONG is older and this is a better fit.

discriminators: A single struck body: sharp attack then a very long ring. HIGH inharmonicity (a bell is the textbook inharmonic resonator) plus a long decay_time_seconds_60db is the split from MUSICAL-PERCUSSION TUNED, which is equally impulsive but strongly harmonic and dies far sooner.
One onset, not many: unlike MUSICAL-CHIME, which is a stochastic cluster of dozens of small strikes with high transient_count and almost no low_band_energy.
Pitched enough to have partials, unlike MUSICAL-PERCUSSION membranes (near-zero harmonicity).
Continuously blown, so the note is held: high envelope_sustain_level and a non-instant envelope_attack_seconds rule out MUSICAL-PLUCKED and MUSICAL-PERCUSSION TUNED.

Brighter and richer than MUSICAL-WOODWIND: lip-buzz excitation puts far more energy in the upper partials, so spectral_centroid_hz, partial_count and high_band_energy all run higher. That brightness axis is the boundary.
Harmonic and stable, unlike MUSICAL-EXPERIMENTAL.

measured_evidence.selection: clips whose FSD50K labels map unambiguously to this one subcategory (high/medium crosswalk confidence only)

measured_evidence.caveat: Amateur Freesound audio, mono, arbitrary normalization. Treat as a prior on the prior, not as truth. Duration, level, stereo width and band limit are NOT measurable here and are left reasoned.

discriminators: Many small strikes, not one: high transient_count with LOW onset_periodicity (wind is stochastic, not metered) is the split from MUSICAL-BELLS (one onset) and from MUSICAL-LOOP (periodic).
Tiny radiating bodies: very high spectral_centroid_hz and almost no low_band_energy, where BELLS and PERCUSSION carry real bass.
Metallic and inharmonic, unlike the clean harmonic bars of MUSICAL-PERCUSSION TUNED.

measured_evidence.caveat: Amateur Freesound audio, mono, arbitrary normalization. Treat as a prior on the prior, not as truth. Duration, level, stereo width and band limit are NOT measurable here and are left reasoned.

discriminators: Voicing is the whole test: a very high voicing_ratio with formant structure separates a choir from every instrumental sustain (MUSICAL-STRINGED, MUSICAL-BRASS, MUSICAL-WOODWIND), which have none.
Sung, not spoken: vowels are held, so envelope_sustain_level and harmonicity are higher and syllabic_modulation_energy lower than in speech.
No periodic instrumental bed, so beats_per_minute stays quiet - unlike MUSICAL-SONG & PHRASE, and unlike MUSICAL-PERFORMANCE, which is a mixed ensemble scene in a room.

explanation: prepared instruments like a prepared piano.

discriminators: A style label over prepared and exotic instruments; no morphology is shared across the class, so signal must never assert it.
The only weak cue is a violated expectation: an instrument-like sound that is unusually inharmonic or unstable for its apparent family. Use it to rank, never to decide.
Prefer MUSICAL-INSTRUMENT or MUSICAL-MISC when the text does not say prepared, exotic or experimental.

explanation: Various instruments that don't have a home in the other MUSICAL categories.

discriminators: Residual bucket for instruments with no home in the other MUSICAL subcategories: it asserts nothing from signal.
Only claim when the text names an instrument that matches no specific sibling (KEYED, PLUCKED, STRINGED, BRASS, WOODWIND, PERCUSSION, PERCUSSION TUNED, BELLS, CHIME, SHAKEN).

explanation: such as pianos and harpsichord. For synthesizer though see MUSICAL-SYNTHESIZED.

discriminators: Hammer-struck strings: fast attack, long harmonic decay, and only SLIGHT inharmonicity (stretched piano partials) - an order of magnitude less than MUSICAL-BELLS.
Signal barely separates a piano from MUSICAL-PLUCKED (same attack/decay shape); text is decisive. An organ instead reads as sustained_tonal, so a high envelope_sustain_level here does not disqualify.
Against MUSICAL-SYNTHESIZED there is no reliable DSP boundary once a sampler is involved - lean on text.

measured_evidence.selection: clips whose FSD50K labels map unambiguously to this one subcategory (high/medium crosswalk confidence only)

measured_evidence.caveat: Amateur Freesound audio, mono, arbitrary normalization. Treat as a prior on the prior, not as truth. Duration, level, stereo width and band limit are NOT measurable here and are left reasoned.

discriminators: The one trivially separable MUSICAL subcategory: a periodic onset train sustained over bars, so beats_per_minute fires and onset_periodicity is high.
One-shots cannot do this - MUSICAL-SAMPLE, MUSICAL-STINGER and MUSICAL-PERCUSSION have too few onsets and no periodicity.
MUSICAL-PERFORMANCE is also long but is a room capture with rubato and low stationarity, not a tight metered cell.

explanation: Music sounds not fitting another category in this list. Hopefully rarely needed.

discriminators: Abstain target for MUSICAL. Emit when the file is clearly musical but no sibling's gates and priors dominate.
Never assert from signal; this exists so the classifier can decline rather than guess.

explanation: including deep timpani or big war drums. Mainly meant for true membrane instruments

provenance.reason: the reasoned gate rejected 6% of this subcategory's own true clips

discriminators: Impulsive AND inharmonic: a struck membrane has no harmonic series, so harmonicity is low, inharmonicity is high, and pitch_hz is unstable or absent.

That inharmonicity axis is the entire boundary against MUSICAL-PERCUSSION TUNED, which shares the identical impulsive envelope but is strongly pitched (high harmonicity, low inharmonicity, a clear pitch_hz).
Short ring on a bass-heavy body: much higher low_band_energy and a far smaller decay_time_seconds_60db than MUSICAL-BELLS.
A single strike, not a grain train: higher crest_factor and lower spectral_flatness than MUSICAL-SHAKEN.

measured_evidence.selection: clips whose FSD50K labels map unambiguously to this one subcategory (high/medium crosswalk confidence only)

discriminators: Mallet-struck bars and plates: impulsive like MUSICAL-PERCUSSION but HARMONIC. High harmonicity, LOW inharmonicity and a clear pitch_hz is the exact axis that separates the two - same envelope, opposite spectrum.
Against MUSICAL-BELLS: a bell is also struck and pitched but is strongly inharmonic and rings far longer; a mallet bar has a much smaller decay_time_seconds_60db and near-harmonic partials.
A stable pitch and low spectral_flatness rule out MUSICAL-SHAKEN and MUSICAL-CHIME.

explanation: high school band. If a mixed ambience

discriminators: A long, multi-instrument musical scene captured in a room: wide stereo_width, low stationarity, minutes rather than seconds.
Less metered than MUSICAL-LOOP (onset_periodicity well below a loop's) and not a mastered mix like MUSICAL-SONG & PHRASE - it carries room tone and audience.
If crowd and room dominate the music, the UCS text sends it to AMBIENCE (PUBLIC PLACE), not here.

explanation: instruments that are mainly plucked when played. See also MUSICAL-STRINGED.

provenance.reason: the reasoned gate rejected 18% of this subcategory's own true clips

discriminators: Impulse excitation of a harmonic string: a tiny envelope_attack_seconds followed by a clean harmonic exponential decay, so envelope_sustain_level is LOW and envelope_temporal_centroid is front-loaded.
That attack/sustain pair is the whole boundary against MUSICAL-STRINGED (bowed: slow attack, high sustain, energy held through the note).
Harmonic decay with low inharmonicity rules out MUSICAL-PERCUSSION; the ring is far shorter and far less inharmonic than MUSICAL-BELLS.

measured_evidence.selection: clips whose FSD50K labels map unambiguously to this one subcategory (high/medium crosswalk confidence only)

measured_evidence.caveat: Amateur Freesound audio, mono, arbitrary normalization. Treat as a prior on the prior, not as truth. Duration, level, stereo width and band limit are NOT measurable here and are left reasoned.

explanation: which has its own subcategory here.

discriminators: A single triggered musical one-shot: the ABSENCE of a periodic onset train (no beats_per_minute, low onset_periodicity) plus a short length is exactly what the UCS text means by 'not loops' - that is the whole boundary against MUSICAL-LOOP.
Otherwise semantic: prefer the specific instrument subcategory whenever the text names one, and MUSICAL-STINGER when the one-shot is a musical accent.

discriminators: Dense stochastic grain rather than a single strike: hundreds of tiny seed/bead collisions, so spectral_flatness is high, crest_factor is LOW, and there is no pitch at all.
Against MUSICAL-PERCUSSION: a drum is one high-crest impulse with real low_band_energy; a shaker is a bright, bass-free noise burst with a micro-onset train.
Zero harmonicity rules out MUSICAL-PERCUSSION TUNED and MUSICAL-CHIME, which are clustered but pitched.

explanation: car music bys. See also MUSICAL-PERFORMANCE which is a better fit for 'live performances'.

discriminators: A complete produced piece: sung vocal (voicing_ratio) sitting on a metered instrumental bed (beats_per_minute, high onset_periodicity) - both together.
Not a bar-length cell, so it is longer and less seamlessly periodic than MUSICAL-LOOP.
A mix, not a room: narrower stereo_width and higher stationarity than MUSICAL-PERFORMANCE, which is a live capture.
Unaccompanied voices with no beat go to MUSICAL-CHORAL.

explanation: A short musical phrase or accent
like a horror 'string' sting. A big pitching cello note. But musical in nature.

discriminators: A musical accent: a front-loaded hit or swell that rings out and stops, a few seconds long and unmetered - no beats_per_minute, which separates it from MUSICAL-LOOP and MUSICAL-SONG & PHRASE.
Musical and harmonic where MUSICAL-PERCUSSION and CARTOON-IMPACT are inharmonic broadband hits.
A stinger is an accent with intent; a bare triggered note is MUSICAL-SAMPLE.

explanation: viola. Harp probably now goes to MUSICAL-PLUCKED.

discriminators: Bowed means continuously excited: a slow envelope_attack_seconds and a HIGH envelope_sustain_level held for the length of the note, with envelope_temporal_centroid near the middle rather than the front.
That attack/sustain pair is precisely the axis against MUSICAL-PLUCKED (instant attack, decaying tail, near-zero sustain) - the same string, a different excitation.
Darker and denser in partials than MUSICAL-BRASS, and bow-noise rather than breath-noise in the attack, unlike MUSICAL-WOODWIND.

measured_evidence.selection: clips whose FSD50K labels map unambiguously to this one subcategory (high/medium crosswalk confidence only)

measured_evidence.caveat: Amateur Freesound audio, mono, arbitrary normalization. Treat as a prior on the prior, not as truth. Duration, level, stereo width and band limit are NOT measurable here and are left reasoned.

explanation: musical synth performances. Some overlap with MUSICAL-SAMPLE
but this meant for more than simple triggered samples.

discriminators: Electronically generated: an unnaturally stable spectrum (high stationarity, low spectral_flux) with no bow, breath or strike noise in the attack, often on a hard-edged waveform (elevated total_harmonic_distortion).
DSP rarely proves synthesis outright - a sampled emulation is indistinguishable - so text is decisive against MUSICAL-KEYED, MUSICAL-BRASS and MUSICAL-STRINGED.
A synth playing a metered pattern over bars is MUSICAL-LOOP; a single triggered note is MUSICAL-SAMPLE.

explanation: also music boxes would go here.

discriminators: A music box: a metered train of pitched, plucked-tine plinks with almost no low_band_energy - a tiny comb and body cannot radiate bass.
Same plucked-bar physics as MUSICAL-PERCUSSION TUNED, but thin, band-limited and clockwork-regular; the missing bass narrows it and text (box, musicbox, jack-in-the-box) commits it.
If the mechanism rattles rather than plays a tune, it is TOYS-MECHANICAL.

Sustained, breath-excited and DARK: the reed/edge tone sits close to a pure harmonic series, so spectral_centroid_hz, partial_count and high_band_energy all fall well below MUSICAL-BRASS. That brightness axis is the boundary.
A held note with a non-instant attack rules out MUSICAL-PLUCKED and MUSICAL-PERCUSSION TUNED.
A near-sinusoidal, very high, gliding tone with almost no partials is a whistle (CARTOON-WHISTLE), not a woodwind.

measured_evidence.selection: clips whose FSD50K labels map unambiguously to this one subcategory (high/medium crosswalk confidence only)
